_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Track and document cyber defense incidents from initial detection through final resolution.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack surfaces, threat actors, and applicable mitigating controls.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-T-007 (DG, cosine: 0.400), RC-T-005 (RC, cosine: 0.369).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.6/10 (raw 0.462). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [cyber, defense, detection, document, final, incidents] vs WIDAI-only terms [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, conduct] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.866, R=0.865). Balanced P/R — the 12-word DCWF element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Track and document cyber defense incidents from initial detection through final" and "Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.407 < STS 0.462 (gap -0.054). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [cyber, defense, detection] and [actors, ai, applicable] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.7%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [cyber, defense, detection, document, final, incidents]. WIDAI terms: [actors, ai, applicable, assessments, attack, conduct].
